mnemonics: REPL It Till You Make It (Read, Execute, Print, Loop)
Remember: Users Never Execute Directly; Shells Interpret Commands (UNED; SIC)
keyPoints: The shell reads a command from standard input.
The shell interprets/parses the command, expanding variables and wildcards.
The shell executes the command by forking a new process and using exec.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that the shell expands wildcards (*, ?) before passing arguments to the command.
Assuming the shell itself executes the command instead of forking a child process to do it.
Not understanding that the shell waits for the command to finish (for foreground jobs) before printing the next prompt.
